text: Access to Justice and Equity in the Ecuadorian Judicial System Through this study it is possible to determine the economic, geographical, cultural and perception barriers that exist and are a barrier for the access to justice. The main barriers that affect access to justice are poverty, absence of judicial infrastructure in rural regions, lack of trust in the judicial system because of the high index of corruption. Furthermore, the large number of proceedings and the bureaucracy of the judicial system affect mainly to low-income families. Some of the recommendations are: - To construct judicial infrastructure in the regions of the country that are further away from capital cities - To implement alternative justice systems in a culturally adapted way, aiming to increase citizen trust in respect to access to justice - To promote transparency and to combat corruption within the judicial system - To carry out an informative campaign in relation to access to justice, in order to allow citizens to be aware of the rights they have within the framework of the judicial system.
headline: Fully Human Written
